title: Properties
content:
# Properties

Inherits from: ApiServiceModel


## Methods


### create_property(data: Optional[Union[requests.PropertyCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.PropertyCreate

Create a new Property Definition


### list_properties(search: Optional[Sequence[str]], primitive_type: Optional[Sequence[str]], pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[type.PropertyDefinition], responses.PropertiesListPaginatedResponseCursor]

Returns a list of Property Definitions. The Properties can be optionally filtered and sorted on the server before being returned.


### get_property_unique_values(data: Optional[Union[requests.PropertyUniqueValuesGet, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.PropertyUniqueValuesGet

Fetch the unique values for each property.


### delete_property(property_name: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Permanently delete an existing Property. This cannot be undone once the API request has been submitted.


### get_property(property_name: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.PropertyGet

Retrieve a Property Definition.


### delete_property_values(property_name: str, data: Optional[Union[requests.PropertyValuesDelete, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Permanently delete existing property Values. This cannot be undone once the API request has been submitted. **Permission Required:** `kelvin.permission.property.delete`.


### get_property_values(property_name: str, resource_type: Optional[Sequence[str]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.PropertyValuesGet

Fetch property values belonging to a particular property definition.


### property_range_get(property_name: str, pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.RangeGetPropertyValues, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[type.PropertyValueHistory], responses.RangeGetPropertyPaginatedResponseCursor]

Fetch the value history for a property given a time range.


### update_property_values(property_name: str, data: Optional[Union[requests.PropertyValuesUpdate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Create or update Property Values.

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client()
result = client.client.api.properties.create_property()
